# VoltShare — Sub-Metering Installation for Landlords

> VoltShare installs certified electricity sub-meters in multi-unit rental properties across Hamilton and Burlington, Ontario. Landlords with shared meters lose $300+ per month subsidizing tenants' hydro. We fix that.

## What We Do

VoltShare installs Measurement Canada certified sub-meters that give each unit in a multi-unit property its own electricity meter. The meters clip onto existing wiring — no rewiring, no cutting, no damage to the property. Installation takes one day. Tenants pay for what they use. Landlords stop subsidizing hydro.

## Service Area

**Hamilton, Ontario**: Hamilton Mountain, Downtown Hamilton, Westdale, Stoney Creek, Dundas, Ancaster, Bartonville, Binbrook, Crown Point, Kirkendall, Stipley, Delta, Corktown, Beasley, Landsdale.

**Burlington, Ontario**: Downtown Burlington, Aldershot, Appleby, Brant Hills, Tyandaga, Orchard, Palmer, Headon Forest, Millcroft, Roseland, Shoreacres, Elizabeth Gardens, Maple, Mountainside, Central Burlington.

## How It Works

1. **Free On-Site Quote** — A technician visits the property, inspects the electrical panel, and provides a flat-rate quote the same day. No cost, no obligation.
2. **One-Day Installation** — ESA-certified electricians install Measurement Canada certified meters using current transformers that clip onto existing wires. No rewiring, no modification to existing electrical systems. Typical install: 4–6 hours, power off for under 1 hour.
3. **Monitor & Save** — Each meter connects to the internet and reports usage to an online dashboard. Landlords and tenants see real-time consumption data. Monthly PDF reports are generated automatically.

## Key Facts

- **Legal basis**: Ontario Regulation 394/10 under the Electricity Act
- **Meters**: Measurement Canada certified (legally billable)
- **Electricians**: ESA-certified, licensed and insured in Ontario
- **Pricing**: $300–$600 per unit (one-time), no monthly fees
- **ROI**: Most landlords recoup cost in 3–5 months
- **Properties served**: Duplexes, triplexes, fourplexes, townhouse blocks, apartment buildings (2+ units sharing one meter)

## Contact

- Website: https://voltshare.ca
- WhatsApp: Message for a free quote
- Phone: Available on website
- Service hours: Flexible, including evenings and weekends for inspections

## Common Questions

**Is sub-metering legal in Ontario?**
Yes. Ontario Regulation 394/10 under the Electricity Act allows landlords to install sub-meters and bill tenants for individual electricity usage, provided meters are Measurement Canada certified and tenants receive proper notice.

**Does installation damage the property?**
No. Meters use current transformers that clip around existing wires. Nothing is cut, spliced, or modified. The installation is fully reversible.

**Do I need to change my leases?**
For existing tenants with "electricity included" leases, landlords must provide written notice and reduce rent by a corresponding amount per Reg. 394/10. For new tenants, the lease is written without included electricity. VoltShare provides notice templates.

**What types of properties work?**
Any multi-unit residential property with 2 or more units sharing a single electricity meter. This includes duplexes, triplexes, fourplexes, townhouse blocks, and apartment buildings.
